subtopic: IUCN World Conservation Congress and Red List
title: IUCN World Conservation Congress and Organizational Overview
focus: Outcomes of the 2025 Congress in Abu Dhabi and the foundational details of the IUCN.
rationale: The Congress resolutions and IUCN's governance structure are distinct, highly testable static and current affairs topics for UPSC.
sectionExcerpt: The IUCN World Conservation Congress 2025 took place in Abu Dhabi, adopting a 20-year Strategic Vision 'Unite for Nature on the Path to 2045'. Key resolutions included a just phase-out of fossil fuels, recognizing wild animals as climate allies, and the first World Summit of Indigenous Peoples and Nature. IUCN, founded in 1948 and headquartered in Gland, Switzerland, is governed by the Members' Assembly and IUCN Council.
evidenceBullets: Congress 2025 held in Abu Dhabi, UAE.
Adopted 'Unite for Nature on the Path to 2045' 20-year Strategic Vision.
First-ever motion advocating for a just phase-out of fossil fuels adopted.
IUCN founded in 1948, located in Gland, Switzerland.
Members' Assembly is the highest governing body, held every 4 years.
Publishes IUCN Report on Protected Areas and IUCN World Heritage Outlook.

subtopic: IUCN World Conservation Congress and Red List
title: India's National Red List Roadmap and Vision 2025-2030
focus: India's 5-year initiative to assess species extinction risks and publish National Red Data Books.
rationale: A specific national initiative with clear targets, nodal agencies, and international alignments, making it a prime candidate for scheme/initiative-based questions.
sectionExcerpt: India unveiled its National Red List Roadmap and Vision 2025-2030 to establish a nationally coordinated red listing system. It is a 5-year initiative aiming to assess the extinction risk of approximately 11,000 species and publish National Red Data Books for flora and fauna by 2030. It is prepared by ZSI and BSI in collaboration with IUCN India and aligns with the CBD and KMGBF.
evidenceBullets: Aims to assess extinction risk of ~11,000 species over 5 years.
Goal to publish National Red Data Books for flora and fauna by 2030.
Nodal Agency: Union Ministry of Environment, Forest & Climate Change.
Prepared by ZSI and BSI in collaboration with IUCN India and Centre for Species Survival.
Internationally aligned with CBD and Kunming-Montreal Global Biodiversity Framework (KMGBF).

subtopic: IUCN World Conservation Congress and Red List
title: IUCN Key Instruments: Red List and Green Status of Species
focus: Details of the IUCN Red List categories, recent species status updates, and the IUCN Green Status framework.
rationale: Species status updates and the distinction between Red List (extinction risk) and Green Status (conservation success) are high-yield areas for UPSC Prelims.
sectionExcerpt: The IUCN Red List of Threatened Species, established in 1964, divides species into 9 categories from Not Evaluated to Extinct. Recent updates uplisted Indian birds like the Indian Courser and Indian Roller to Near Threatened, and the Long-billed Grasshopper-warbler to Endangered. The IUCN Green Status of Species, launched in 2021, complements the Red List by measuring conservation success and recovery across 8 categories, using a Green Score from 0 to 100%.
evidenceBullets: IUCN Red List divides species into 9 categories (NE, DD, LC, NT, VU, EN, CR, EW, EX).
Indian Courser, Indian Roller, and Rufous-tailed Lark uplisted to Near Threatened.
Green Sea Turtle improved from Endangered to Least Concern.
IUCN Green Status of Species launched in 2021 to measure conservation success, complementing the Red List.
Green Status includes 8 categories and a Green Score (0-100%) indicating proximity to full recovery.
